name: Conti Ransomware Intrusion
description: Faithful ordering of a Conti human-operated ransomware intrusion: access via brute-forced/valid credentials, Cobalt Strike beacon execution, Mimikatz LSASS dumping, RDP lateral movement, scheduled-task + backdoor-account persistence, and Defender disablement just before encryption. Dwell times are compressed for a live demo (Conti operators typically dwell hours-to-days between phases); the ordering and causal chain are faithful.

kill_chain.note: RDP/credential brute force to obtain a foothold
Encoded PowerShell loads a Cobalt Strike beacon
Mimikatz / comsvcs MiniDump of LSASS for domain creds
RDP to high-value hosts using harvested credentials
Scheduled task re-launches the beacon on reboot
Backdoor local account that masquerades as a service account
Disable Microsoft Defender immediately before encryption
